chapters.title: Keigo — Formal Japanese
chapters.description: Master sonkeigo (respectful) and kenjougo (humble) speech levels.

lessons.prompt: What is the sonkeigo (respectful) form of 食べる (to eat)?
lessons.explanation: 'Meshiagaru' is the respectful form of eating — used when describing what the other person/someone respected eats. 'Itadaku' is humble (what you eat).

lessons.prompt: 社長はもうお帰り___なりました。(The president has already gone home.)
lessons.explanation: 'O + verb stem + ni naru' = respectful expression. 'Okaeri ni narimashita' = has gone home (respectful).

lessons.prompt: Translate humbly: 'I will come to your office tomorrow'
lessons.correctAnswer: 明日お宅にうかがいます
lessons.hint: 'Ukagau' is the humble form of 'iku' (go) and 'kuru' (come). Used when you go/come toward a superior.

lessons.prompt: What is the humble form of 'もらう' (to receive)?
lessons.explanation: 'Itadaku' = humble form of morau (receive). 'Kudasaru' = respectful form of kureru (someone gives to you).

lessons.prompt: ご説明___してもよろしいでしょうか。(May I explain?)
lessons.explanation: 'Go-setsumei o shite mo yoroshii deshou ka' = May I explain? 'Yoroshii deshou ka' is very polite permission-asking.

learningMaterial.title: Keigo — Formal Japanese
learningMaterial.intro: Master sonkeigo (respectful) and kenjougo (humble) speech levels.

concepts.explanation: 'Meshiagaru' is the respectful form of eating — used when describing what the other person/someone respected eats. 'Itadaku' is humble (what you eat).
'O + verb stem + ni naru' = respectful expression. 'Okaeri ni narimashita' = has gone home (respectful).

concepts.term: 明日お宅にうかがいます
concepts.explanation: 'Ukagau' is the humble form of 'iku' (go) and 'kuru' (come). Used when you go/come toward a superior.
'Itadaku' = humble form of morau (receive). 'Kudasaru' = respectful form of kureru (someone gives to you).
'Go-setsumei o shite mo yoroshii deshou ka' = May I explain? 'Yoroshii deshou ka' is very polite permission-asking.

chapters.title: Advanced Grammar Patterns
chapters.description: Master complex N1/N2 level grammar patterns for fluent expression.

lessons.prompt: What does 〜にもかかわらず mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Ni mo kakawarazu' = despite/in spite of. Formal written Japanese. '雨にもかかわらず' = despite the rain.

lessons.prompt: 努力した___、失敗してしまった。(Despite having made effort, I ended up failing.)
lessons.explanation: 'Doryoku shita ni mo kakawarazu, shippai shite shimatta.' High-level contrast expression.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'No matter how hard I try, I can't do it'
lessons.correctAnswer: どんなに頑張っても、できない
lessons.hint: 'Donna ni ~ te mo' = no matter how much. 'Ganbatte mo' = even if I try hard.

lessons.prompt: What nuance does 〜わけにはいかない add?
lessons.explanation: 'Wake ni wa ikanai' = cannot (due to social obligation or logical reason). '嘘をつくわけにはいかない' = I can't lie (it's not something I can do).

lessons.prompt: 今さら___わけにはいかない。(I can't give up now — after coming this far.)
lessons.explanation: 'Akiraeru wake ni wa ikanai' = cannot give up (given the situation). 'Imasara' = at this point/after all this.

learningMaterial.title: Advanced Grammar Patterns
learningMaterial.intro: Master complex N1/N2 level grammar patterns for fluent expression.

concepts.explanation: 'Ni mo kakawarazu' = despite/in spite of. Formal written Japanese. '雨にもかかわらず' = despite the rain.
'Doryoku shita ni mo kakawarazu, shippai shite shimatta.' High-level contrast expression.

concepts.term: どんなに頑張っても、できない
concepts.explanation: 'Donna ni ~ te mo' = no matter how much. 'Ganbatte mo' = even if I try hard.
'Wake ni wa ikanai' = cannot (due to social obligation or logical reason). '嘘をつくわけにはいかない' = I can't lie (it's not something I can do).
'Akiraeru wake ni wa ikanai' = cannot give up (given the situation). 'Imasara' = at this point/after all this.

chapters.title: Japanese Idioms & Proverbs
chapters.description: Understand native expressions and four-character idioms (yojijukugo).

lessons.prompt: What does '七転び八起き (nana korobi ya oki)' mean?
options: Fall seven times, stand up eight (perseverance)
Sleep seven times, wake eight times
Try seven methods, succeed on eighth
lessons.correctAnswer: Fall seven times, stand up eight (perseverance)
lessons.explanation: Famous proverb about perseverance and resilience. Fall seven times but always get up — equivalent of 'if at first you don't succeed'.

lessons.prompt: 一石___鳥 (isseki ___ chou) — Kill two birds with one stone
lessons.explanation: 'Isseki nichou' (一石二鳥) = one stone, two birds. A four-character idiom (yojijukugo) meaning to achieve two things at once.

lessons.prompt: What does the yojijukugo '一期一会' (ichi-go ichi-e) mean?
lessons.hint: A philosophy from tea ceremony culture — treasure each meeting as it may never happen again.

lessons.explanation: 'Sasuga' = as expected (of someone impressive). 'Sasuga desu ne' = as I'd expect from you (genuine compliment).

lessons.prompt: 石の___にも三年 — Perseverance leads to mastery (stone warmer proverb)
lessons.explanation: 'Ishi no ue ni mo sannen' (石の上にも三年) = even on a cold stone, three years (makes it warm). Means persistence pays off.

concepts.term: Fall seven times, stand up eight (perseverance)

'Isseki nichou' (一石二鳥) = one stone, two birds. A four-character idiom (yojijukugo) meaning to achieve two things at once.
A philosophy from tea ceremony culture — treasure each meeting as it may never happen again.
'Sasuga' = as expected (of someone impressive). 'Sasuga desu ne' = as I'd expect from you (genuine compliment).
'Ishi no ue ni mo sannen' (石の上にも三年) = even on a cold stone, three years (makes it warm). Means persistence pays off.
